transcript:
What you're seeing is a dense sphere of a thousand-plus glowing points knit together by thousands of shimmering lines — it starts electric blue, then floods slowly into a white-hot core, breathing in and out as it rotates.

Here's the part I love: near the top of the code, in the "dimensional constants," every node gets sixteen close connections but also eight "distant connections" — the comment calls it "attention reaching across context," deliberately linking near and far tokens the same way. And in the update loop, there's an actual pressure system — "attention pressure" builds over a twenty-two second "saturation cycle" until it hits one and triggers a "discharge," flinging bursts of brightness through eighty random connections at once. It's not just decorative pulsing — it's built as accumulation and release, tension and snap.

So that slow brightening you're watching isn't just a fade — it's the network approaching a threshold. What looks like organic breathing is actually code counting toward a small crisis, over and over.
